free_text: Comments, curves and fission
width values are given.   (In English)
Cross-section values given on
sheets.
-Measurement of the neutron capture and fission
cross-sections of Am-241 -
Facility details.
(In English)
Harwell 45 MeV electron accelerator, running at
repetition frequency of 192 MHz.
Neutron booster target of the Harwell LINAC.
-Am-241 sample was approximately 12 grams of AmO2
giving an N-value for the Am-241 of 1.32*10**-3
atoms/barn.
-It was canned in nickel and aluminum each having a
total thickness of 0.4 mm.
-An identical empty can was used to evaluate the
contribution of the canning material to the
background.
-The major impurities were plutonium isotopes (1.5 %
by weight) of which about half was Pu-239.
-Samples were mounted in a double sleeve of boron and
lead to reduce the effects of scattered neutrons and
soft gamma-rays at the detector.
- U-235 sample of 3.8E-03 atoms/barn for fission
measurement only.
Comparison with other data.
Data taken from 77KIEV and private
communication, 1978-09-23.
G.C., MONITOR reaction strings corrected,
error analyses changed.
M.M. BIB and COMMON information was added.
SUBENT .002 REACTION code was changed from "(N,G)"
into "(N,ABS)". MONITORs values were corrected in
according to data from text of 1st reference.
Average neutron
absorption cross-section (predominantly capture).
Gamma-yield measurements were carried out for
both Am-241 and the blank sample holder with a
scintillator situated at the 100 meter flight path.
-The accelerator pulse width was 0.5 micro-sec and
the nominal resolution of 5.8 nano-sec/meter.
-A 6 mm lead sleeve was placed around the sample to
reduce the soft gamma-ray pule up.
-A bias of 3 MeV was imposed to the detector.
-The detector was only used in the coincidence mode.
-Time-of-flight data were accumulated for a fixed
pulse-height window of 3 MeV to 7 MeV using a digital
time analyzer width 1/4 micro-sec timing channel and
the ability to accept several events per accelerator
cycle.
-The shape of the absorption c.s. was determined
using the same sample in the range 100 eV to 500 keV.
In this case capture gamma-ray events were detected
with a large liquid scintillator.
"Black resonance" technique with aluminum and
manganese filters - to determine the time-independent
component of background. Shape and magnitude of this
background was found to be almost identical with yield
obtained for blank sample holder in region < 150 keV.
230 l Large liquid scintillator.
Detector efficiency is the same for capture and
fission events.
Shape of incident neutron spectrum was determined by
comparison with Li-6(n,a) cross-section below 30 keV
and U-235(n,f) cross-section at higher energies.
Cross-section was normalized in energy range form 1keV
to 2keV to value of 9.48b from MONIT-REF.
Capture gammas.
Data taken from 77KIEV and private
communication, 1978-09-23.
G.C., MONITOR reaction strings corrected,
error analysis changed.
HH REACTION/MONITOR string corrected
-For time-dependent and constant background.
-For sample thickness effects less 3%.
-For multiple scattering and self shielding effects
from 4.5 % at 100 eV to 12% at 500keV.
-For detector efficiency.
Statistical error corresponding to one standard
deviation (12%).
This error does not include any uncertainty in the
assumed average cross-section in the 1 to 2 keV
energy interval where the data were normalized.
Uncertainty in determining the shape of incident
neutron flux less 3%.
Fission yields measurements were made for the
Am-241 and U-235 samples, using a 13 m flight path.
Pulse shape discrimination against gammas
-The U-235 measurements were used to establish an
absolute scale for the Am-241 fission cross-section.
-In each case the electron pulse length was 150 nsec,
giving a nominal resolution of nsec/meter.
-Fission events were detected by observing prompt
fission neutrons with a proton recoil detector placed
to one side of the sample but outside the neutron
beam.
-Measurements with a gold sample were used to check
the amount of breakthrough.
-The time-of-flight information (minimum channel
width 1/16 microsec.)was collected and stored by an
on-line computer system capable of accepting several
events peR LINAC cycle.
-The time dependent background was determined by the
black resonance filter technique using samples of
manganese,tantalum and titanium.
Background were measured for two thicknesses of each
material to enable extrapolation to zero thickness.
-A permanent cadmium overlap filter was present in all
measurements.
-The time constant background was determined by
recording events at the end of each accelerator
cycle with a background gate.
-The spectrum of incident neutrons was measured
relative to the Li-6(N,ALPHA) cross-section, using a
lithium glass detector. This detector was placed in
the same position as the sample in the fission yield
measurements and background were determined in an
identical manner using the same filters.
-Fission cross-sections were measured in the energy
range 1 eV to 10 keV  by observing prompt fission
neutrons.
Lithium glass detector, monitoring the
incident neutron spectrum.
Four NE-213 proton recoil detectors placed
around the sample, out of the incident neutron beam,
with a pulse shape discrimination system.
This system was used to distinguish the neutron and
gamma-ray events which were then stored as separate
time-of-flight spectra.
-A pulse height bias equivalent to 1.5 MeV neutron
energy was applied in all measurements.
-The observed time-of-flight spectra for both
the Am-241 and the U-235 were converted to fission
cross-section shapes after correction for background
and incident neutron spectrum energy dependence.
-The U-235 cross-section was placed on an absolute
scale by normalizing the average U-235 fission yield
in the 1 keV to 2 keV range of incident neutron
energy to the value of 7.167 barns given by a recent
status report on the U-235 evaluation in MONIT-REF.
-The Am-241 fission c.s. was then normalized in the
same energy range using the calibration obtained
from the U-235 measurement, and the difference in
the value of nu-bar for Am-241 and U-235, was taken
into account,using the values of Manero and Konshin.
To determine absolute
scale for Am-241 fission cross section.
Li-6(n,a) reaction was used
to measure incident neutron spectrum.
U-235 evaluated c-s,
used for normalization.  ERDA-NDC-5/L.
Values of prompt
neutrons nu-bar for Am-241 and U-235, used in
normalization procedure. Value of average nu-bar for
Pu-239, used for correction.
Evaluated Pu-239
fission cross-section, used in correction for Pu-239
contribution (above 100 eV).Between 50 and 100 eV the
data of U.K. Data Library were used.
Prompt neutrons.
Data taken from 77KIEV and private
communication, 1978-09-23. Presented on Fig.1 of
reference.
G.C., MONITOR reaction strings corrected,
error analysis changed.
-For background.
-For energy dependence of the incident neutron
spectrum.
-For the difference in the value of NU-BAR For Am-241
and U-235.
-For contribution from the Pu-239 impurity (typical
contribution was 50% in 100eV region and less 20% at
10keV).
-Finite sample corrections for self-shielding and
multiple scattering made by the Monte Carlo method,
varied from 4.5% in low energy range to maximum 9% in
the few keV region.
-For detector efficiency.
Statistical error corresponding to one standard
deviation  ( 25.%).
This error does not include any uncertainty in the
assumed average cross-section in the 1 to 2 keV
energy interval where the data were normalized.
Uncertainties in measuring the shape of
incident neutron flux, in normalizing the
cross-section, and in making the sample thickness
correction, were all less about 3.% .
See SUBENTRY 20785002 and 20785003.
-Based on the assumption that to a first approximation
the ratio,R,of fission neutron to gamma-ray yield for
a particular resonance is given by:
*     R = E(F,N)*NU-BAR(P)*G(F)/                    *
where E(F,N) is the detector efficiency for fis. n.,
E(C,G) and E(F,G) the overall efficiencies for
detecting capture and fission gamma-rays,
G(F),G(G) are fission and capture widths.
-Authors took as good approximation the following
value:
G(F) = C*G(G)*(R/NU)
where C is about 2., obtained by calibration from
U-235 data.
Total radiation width
assumed to be constant and equal to 40 milli-eV.
Same assumption as above.
Values of average
NU-BAR, used in analysis .
Data Taken From 77KIEV and private
communication, 1978-09-23.
Table 1 of 1st reference.
G.C., Error analysis changed.
Largest source of error was a systematic one
due to normalization and was thought to be 15 %.
